2604.09237.title: ScheMatiQ: From Research Question to Structured Data through Interactive Schema Discovery
2604.09237.paper_summary: Many disciplines pose natural-language research questions over large document collections whose answers typically require structured evidence, traditionally obtained by manually designing an annotation schema and exhaustively labeling the corpus, a slow and error-prone process. We introduce ScheMatiQ, which leverages calls to a backbone LLM to take a question and a corpus to produce a schema and a grounded database, with a web interface that lets steer and revise the extraction. In collaboration with domain experts, we show that ScheMatiQ yields outputs that support real-world analysis in law and computational biology. We release ScheMatiQ as open source with a public web interface, and invite experts across disciplines to use it with their own data. All resources, including the website, source code, and demonstration video, are available at: www.ScheMatiQ-ai.com
2604.09237.paper_summary_zh: 許多學科提出自然語言研究問題，針對大型文檔集合，其答案通常需要結構化證據，這些證據傳統上是通過手動設計註釋架構並徹底標註語料庫來獲得，這是一個緩慢且容易出錯的過程。<lb/>我們介紹了ScheMatiQ，它利用對主幹LLM的調用，將問題和語料庫結合起來，生成一個架構和一個基於實證的數據庫，並提供一個網頁界面，讓用戶能夠引導和修訂提取過程。<lb/>與領域專家合作，我們展示了ScheMatiQ產出的結果支持法律和計算生物學中的現實世界分析。<lb/>我們將ScheMatiQ作為開源軟體發布，並提供公共網頁界面，邀請各學科的專家使用自己的數據。<lb/>所有資源，包括網站、源代碼和演示視頻，均可在：www.ScheMatiQ-ai.com 獲得。
2604.09237.author: Shahar Levy et.al.
2604.09237.authors: Shahar Levy, Eliya Habba, Reshef Mintz, Barak Raveh, Renana Keydar, Gabriel Stanovsky
